title: On Sensibility. To My Dear And Much Honoured Friend, Mrs. Dunlop, Of Dunlop.
content:
Sensibility how charming,
            Thou, my friend, canst truly tell:
        But distress with horrors arming,
            Thou host also known too well.

        Fairest flower, behold the lily,
            Blooming in the sunny ray:
        Let the blast sweep o'er the valley,
            See it prostrate on the clay.

        Hear the woodlark charm the forest,
            Telling o'er his little joys:
        Hapless bird! a prey the surest,
            To each pirate of the skies.

        Dearly bought, the hidden treasure,
            Finer feeling can bestow;
        Chords that vibrate sweetest pleasure,
            Thrill the deepest notes of woe.
meaning:
Robert Burns's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "On Sensibility. To My Dear And Much Honoured Friend, Mrs. Dunlop, Of Dunlop."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
